title: Steven Fenyves, CFP, CFS - Valued Wealth Management Inc.
name: Steven Fenyves, CFP, CFS
details:
📍 5255 North Federal Highway, Suite 330, Boca Raton, FL 33487

**Why:**
✅ Solo founder (no partnership complexity)
✅ 21 years = mature practice, peak value
✅ Independent BD affiliation (Osaic) = portable book
✅ Boca Raton = strong market, retiree-focused
✅ No evidence of succession plan on website

**Succession:** 21 years in practice | Solo practice with small team | Named after service ("Valued Wealth")

**Valuation:** - **Conservative (2x revenue):** $1M-3M
- **Fair market (2.5x):** $1.25M-3.75M
- **Aggressive (3x):** $1.5M-4.5M

title: Mauro Guerra - Guerra Wealth Advisors
name: Mauro Guerra
details:
📍 Miami, FL

**Why:**
✅ Patriarch with succession question (will sons fully take over or need partner?)
✅ No PE backing or aggregator affiliation
✅ 40-year milestone = natural transition point
✅ Family businesses often need outside succession when next gen is uncertain

**Succession:** 40 years in practice | Family succession in progress | "Thousands of clients"

**Valuation:** - **Conservative (2x revenue):** $12M
- **Fair market (2.5x):** $15M
- **Aggressive (3x):** $18M

title: Victor Delerme, CPA - Delerme CPA
name: Victor Delerme, CPA
details:
📍 Brickell, Miami, FL

**Client Focus:**
- High-income business owners ($500K+ annual income)
- Global entrepreneurs with international footprint
- Puerto Rico Act 60 tax optimization specialists
- Multi-entity structuring for tax reduction
- Bilingual team (English/Spanish)

**Why:**
✅ Explicitly markets to "high-income business owners, investors, and global entrepreneurs"
✅ Strategic tax planning focus (not just compliance)
✅ Act 60 expertise = clients considering major wealth moves
✅ **No mention of advisor partnerships on website or LinkedIn**
✅ Bilingual = Miami Latin American market overlap
approach: Victor, I came across your work with high-income entrepreneurs and Act 60 relocations—impressive niche. I'm a wealth advisor in Miami working with similar clients (business owners scaling 7-8 figures, often with international exposure). I'm finding that most of my clients' CPAs focus on compliance, not strategy. You clearly do both. That's rare. I'm curious if you've built referral relationships with advisors who complement your tax work—estate planning, investment structuring, insurance optimization, etc. Many of my CPA relationships are informal, but the best ones are intentional. Would you be open to a quick call to explore whether there's a fit? Not looking to pitch you—just comparing notes on how we each serve this market.

title: Edgar Gomez, CPA, MST, MBA - E. Gomez CPA LLC
name: Edgar Gomez, CPA, MST, MBA
details:
📍 Brickell, Miami, FL

**Client Focus:**
- Construction contractors (project-based businesses)
- Freight & trucking companies
- Wholesale distributors
- Tax resolution and audit protection
- QuickBooks/cloud accounting implementation

**Why:**
✅ 20+ years = mature client relationships
✅ Big 4 background = sophisticated technical skills
✅ Focus on business owners with complex operations (construction, freight = high cash flow)
✅ Tax resolution practice = clients with financial stress (opportunity for wealth planning intervention)
✅ **No advisor partnership mentioned on website**
approach: Edgar, I saw you've built a strong practice with construction and freight businesses in Miami—impressive niche. Those clients have unique cash flow challenges (I know, I work with several). I'm a wealth advisor here, and I'm finding that business owners in those industries often have great tax planning (if they have a good CPA like you), but terrible wealth accumulation strategies. They're profitable, but they're not building personal wealth outside the business. I'm curious if you've seen that pattern—and whether you've built referral relationships with advisors who help your clients translate business success into personal financial security. Not looking to pitch you anything. Just wondering if there's a fit to collaborate where your tax work ends and wealth planning begins.

title: Edward D. Quilca, CPA - Quilca CPA Group
name: Edward D. Quilca, CPA
details:
📍 Miami, FL

**Client Focus:**
- Startups (early-stage entrepreneurs)
- Real estate investors (buy-and-hold, flips, 1031 exchanges)
- High-net-worth individuals
- International tax planning
- Small business accounting

**Why:**
✅ FIU graduate (local Miami roots)
✅ 500+ LinkedIn connections = well-networked
✅ Real estate investors AND HNW individuals = wealth accumulation clients
✅ Startup advisory = entrepreneurs experiencing liquidity events
✅ **No mention of advisor partnerships**
approach: Edward, I saw you work with real estate investors and startup founders in Miami—two groups I work with constantly. Both have similar blind spots: they're great at building wealth, terrible at protecting and diversifying it. Real estate guys have 100% of their net worth in property. Startup founders have 100% in equity. Both need someone to help them translate that into long-term financial security—but most CPAs don't want to touch that (and most advisors don't understand their tax situations). I'm curious if you've built partnerships with advisors who understand your clients' worlds. I'm not looking to compete with you—I'm looking for CPAs who can help me serve clients better, and vice versa. Worth a quick call to explore?

title: Alvaro Acevedo Jr., JD, CPA - Brickell Accountants
name: Alvaro Acevedo Jr., JD, CPA
details:
📍 800 Brickell Ave, Miami, FL

**Client Focus:**
- International business owners (U.S. inbound and outbound)
- Foreign investors in U.S. real estate (FIRPTA compliance)
- Executives of multinational companies
- High-net-worth individuals with cross-border wealth
- FATCA compliance specialists

**Why:**
✅ **Attorney + CPA** = sophisticated estate and tax planning conversations
✅ **Since 1980** = 44+ years, massive relationship network
✅ Brickell location = financial district, corporate clients
✅ FIRPTA expertise = foreign nationals buying U.S. real estate ($2M+ deals)
✅ **No wealth advisor partnership** despite serving ultra-HNW international clients
approach: Alvaro, I saw you've been serving international clients in Brickell since 1980—that's a serious track record. I'm a wealth advisor here, and I keep running into foreign nationals who have great tax and legal structuring (usually through a firm like yours) but zero investment strategy or wealth planning. They're compliant. They're structured. But they're not optimized for long-term wealth growth. I'm curious if you've seen that pattern—and whether you've built referral relationships with advisors who can help your clients with the investment/wealth planning side after you handle the tax/legal side. Not looking to pitch you. Just wondering if there's a fit to collaborate on the international client segment. Worth a call?

title: Mongio & Associates, CPAs
name: Mongio & Associates, CPAs
details:
📍 1100 S Miami Ave, Miami, FL (Brickell area)

**Client Focus:**
- Business owners (tax, bookkeeping, consulting)
- IRS problem resolution (tax debt, audits, appeals)
- Expert witness testimony (financial litigation)
- Financial modeling and coaching
- Military veteran-focused services

**Why:**
✅ **IRS problem resolution** = clients in financial distress (opportunity for wealth reset/planning)
✅ **Financial coaching** mentioned = already stepping into advisory territory
✅ Virtual firm = modern, tech-forward (likely open to new partnerships)
✅ "Save time, money, reduce stress" marketing = relationship-oriented
✅ **No wealth advisor partnership mentioned**
approach: [Partner Name], I noticed Mongio & Associates specializes in IRS problem resolution—that's a unique and valuable niche. I'm a wealth advisor in Miami, and I'm curious about something: What happens to your clients AFTER you fix their tax problems? They're compliant again, but do they have a plan to rebuild wealth and avoid ending up back in the same position? I work with business owners who've been through financial crises (tax debt, business failures, divorces). The ones who succeed long-term have two things: a great CPA (to keep them compliant) and a wealth advisor (to help them rebuild smart). I'm wondering if there's a fit to collaborate—not on the tax resolution side (that's your expertise), but on the 'what comes next' side for clients you've helped turn around. Worth a conversation?
